Partir — Short Documentary

2011 · Documentary Short

Partir

Writer & Director

A personal documentary built from photographs, old Super 8 footage, and interviews — a portrait of my father's history. Official selection at Havana Film Festival and Chicago Latino Film Festival. Winner at Rengo and Providencia Film Festivals.

Havana Film Festival Best Short — Rengo Film Festival Jury Special Mention — Providencia FF Chicago Latino Film Festival

A Decorous Woman

Executive Producer

Co-production with Parina Films (Chile) and Tomsa Films (France). 70% of budget secured through grants. Selected for Malaga Fund & Co-production Market 2025 and Cinélatino Toulouse.

Malaga Fund Market 2025 Chile + France Co-production Cinélatino Toulouse
02 — Experimental New Media

Frank &
Somewhere Island

An interactive sci-fi visual story — written entirely by me, with ChatGPT as an editing tool — released episodically on Discord. The audience was split into rival factions following parallel timelines; community votes shaped the plot after each episode.

The visual pipeline was genuinely hybrid: AI generation (Midjourney, video tools, After Effects) combined with handmade animation from collaborating animators, plus a 3D body scan that physically grounds the character in the real world. The result lived somewhere between cinema, gaming, and community art.

The story culminated in 222 Bitcoin ordinals on Magic Eden. The full collection sold out in three minutes. Beyond the collection, what excited me most was the community that formed: fan theories, meme channels, members posting their own Frank artwork — a living world around the story.

222Ordinals Inscribed
3′Sold Out In
$200KRevenue
3 mo.Start to Finish

Season 2 in development — upgrading to cinematic interactive format. Visual direction: Under the Skin (Glazer) × 2001 (Kubrick). Runway ML central to the pipeline.

Somewhere Island → Frank on Magic Eden →
Season 2 — Visual Direction

Post-human body horror held in existential stillness. Toxic acid-green atmospheres — infrastructure, not nature. Silhouetted figures at thresholds, dark liquid seeping as transformation, geological scale dwarfing the individual. The test frames below are early pipeline outputs.

Aesthetic References

Under the Skin — Jonathan Glazer
2001: A Space Odyssey — Stanley Kubrick

Frank Season 1 — Official Teaser
Season 1 — The Collection

222 pieces inscribed as Bitcoin ordinals — the full collection sold out on Magic Eden in 3 minutes. Each visual piece emerged from the community-driven narrative: AI generation, handmade animation, and found footage processed through a shared story that the audience shaped in real time.

Frank Season 1 — 222 Ordinals / Complete Collection

Making of Frank S1
Ordinals Collection on Magic Eden
Weird Guy — Character Exploration
Community Response
Weird Guy Mural — Fan Art, Europe
The Community

One of the project's followers painted a Weird Guy mural in Europe — unsolicited, unprompted. The community that formed around Frank wasn't an audience; it was a world. Fan theories, meme channels, member-created artwork. The story belonged to them as much as it did to us.

Season 2 — Development Footage
Interface Test
Character Exploration
Screen Test
Season 2 — Character Development

The boards below are part of the active writing and world-building process for Season 2. Each character has a defined arc, visual language, and role in the dual-timeline narrative — developed by me as writer and co-creator, translated into visual references for the production pipeline.

Characters 01

Frank — half-human, present timeline. Weird Guy — his future hybrid self. Core form, outfit explorations, mask concepts.

Characters 02

Mother — deity figure, multiform. Core form, states, pure god revealed. Can merge with nature; hair transforms like Medusa.
